- By Authors - All investigated nuclides have
short-lived parents and thus corrections had to be made
for growth from the parent during the irradiation
In each case, two uranium targets were irradiated at
each energy with appropriately chosen periods of
irradiations and periods elapsing between the end of
bombardment and separation to optimize these
corrections.  Both targets were processed identically
and the activities measured under identical conditions.
It is estimated probable error. The error arise
from uncertainties in weighing, pipetting, decay curve
determination and uncertainties in monitor cross
